title: [instcombine] Pull sext/zext through reduce.or/and
summary:
The developers generalized the pattern by separating two distinct optimizations: (1) hoisting `sext`/`zext` out of `reduce_and`/`reduce_or`, and (2) canonicalizing i1 vector reductions to integer bitcast + comparison.

Originally, the code only handled i1 vectors with extensions via `m_ZExtOrSExtOrSelf`. The patch splits this: first, it matches `m_ZExtOrSExt` to pull extensions outside the reduction, enabling the transform for any element type (e.g., i8 to i64). This is applied before the i1 canonicalization, which now only runs on i1 vectors without extensions.

The key insight is decoupling the concerns: extension hoisting works for any vector type, while i1 canonicalization is a separate, specialized rule. This makes the optimization more modular and reusable—developers can apply similar reasoning by isolating type-specific rewrites from general operand transformations.
